items.question: What does the Plotly topic own vs Matplotlib and Seaborn?
items.answer: Plotly owns interactive figures (Plotly Express, Graph Objects), hover tooltips, HTML export, and Dash dashboard patterns for ML. Matplotlib owns static Figure/Axes and savefig; Seaborn owns statistical plotting defaults on Matplotlib—you pick Plotly for exploration and HTML, Matplotlib/Seaborn for static deliverables.

items.question: When do you reach for Graph Objects instead of Plotly Express?
items.answer: Use GO when you need explicit trace assembly—mixed bar and line on one axes, custom hovertemplate with customdata, dual y-axes, or make_subplots with manual row/col placement. Express covers tidy DataFrame scatter, line, histogram, and facet EDA in fewer lines.

items.question: How do you add per-point ML metadata to hover tooltips?
items.answer: In Express, pass hover_data=['sample_id', 'y_true', 'y_pred']. In GO, set customdata to parallel arrays and a hovertemplate with %{customdata[i]} placeholders. Without these, hover shows only mapped x/y/color fields.

items.question: How do you build epoch-aligned loss and accuracy subplots?
items.answer: from plotly.subplots import make_subplots; fig = make_subplots(rows=2, cols=1, shared_xaxes=True); add go.Scatter traces per metric to the correct row; fig.update_layout(hovermode='x unified') so one epoch hover shows all series in that panel group.

items.question: How does Plotly relate to embedding visualization?
items.answer: Plotly provides interactive 2D/3D scatter and rich hover for coordinate data—px.scatter_3d or go.Scatter3d. Embedding visualization owns interpretation of UMAP/t-SNE/PCA layouts, color semantics, and publication guardrails, not the trace API itself.
